The Dawning Archaic: ALSA 1.9 / 85% play screams free first-pick, but the 53.7% GIH WR and -47% OH are damning: it's a colorless top-end you slam everywhere yet it rarely swings games. Take it P1P1 for flexibility, but it's a payoff, not a bomb — soup spell-count keeps it merely playable.
Emeritus of Truce: Honestly elite: 58.6% GIH plus a 59% OH means it pulls weight on the play, not just topdecked. Clean WW cost transfers fine to any white deck — the conditional StP is live often enough. Premium white pick, better than the cube-cynicism implies for Limited.
Practiced Offense: Believe the hype — 64.7% GIH and a monstrous 9.7 IWD make this the white finisher of the format. It's colorless-castable WW so the WR transfers honestly to ANY proactive deck (Silverquill, Lorehold). First-pick, game-ending with any evasion. Take it over most bombs.
Summoned Dromedary: Negative IWD (-1.2) and 4.8 ALSA expose this as pure filler: the 54% GIH is the soup floor for a body that doesn't move games. Fine roleplayer/curve-filler in any white deck, never a pick you're happy about. Late, replaceable.
Flow State: The 59% GD vs 53% OH split is the tell — it's a build-around card-advantage/tempo spell that overperforms once you're set up, not a tempo play. Strong UR/Prismari control fuel given that's the spell-density shell; solid mid-pick, weaker in aggressive decks.
Muse's Encouragement: 35% play rate and 7.2 ALSA say the field correctly fades it; 48% OH means it's dead weight early. A reactive blue common that's only a 13th-15th card in spell-heavy Prismari. Sideboard/last-resort filler, not a maindeck staple.
Arcane Omens: The experts are right — this is a soup-mirror bomb, and the 59.7% GIH / 8.8 IWD is genuinely soup-CONDITIONED (Converge X=4-5 makes it a snap-concede). In a clean 2-color Prismari it discards 1-2 and underwhelms. Speculate late, prioritize only when committed to 4-5c.
Grave Researcher: Trust LoL's pushback: 1.4 IWD and a flat 55% with no OH/GD gap mean it's a glorified Centaur Courser that wants a sacrifice/feed shell SOS doesn't really support. Witherbloom is the format's worst archetype, so this rare is solid filler at best, not a payoff.
Ral Zarek, Guest Lecturer: A planeswalker that plays like a roleplayer — 54.6% GIH and 2.2 IWD are mediocre for a mythic PW because his Witherbloom/Silverquill midrange home is shallow. Take it as a value engine pick, but it's no game-warper; closer to filler than bomb.
Blazing Firesinger: Sub-50% GIH and negative IWD confirm the scrap verdict — exactly the 2-toughness red 'prowess' creature the dead UR-aggro deck wanted. 39% play rate is the field agreeing. Sideboard/unplayable; don't get baited into the aggro that never materialized.
Living History: 56% GIH but -1.2 IWD and 5.5 ALSA mark this as a value enchantment that's nice-to-have, not need-to-have — the negative IWD says decks win fine without it. Best as a grindy Lorehold/graveyard-value engine. Mid-pick role-player, replaceable late.
Tackle Artist: Classic filler common: 1.0 IWD, 6.5 ALSA, 47% play. The 53% GD vs 51% OH says it's slightly better when behind. A late red body for any deck needing creature count; take it 12th-15th, never reach.
Emeritus of Abundance: The real deal — 59.2% GIH with a strong 8.0 IWD and clean GG cost that transfers to any green deck. 2G Regrowth-on-a-body is premium green value that grinds soup mirrors. First-pickable, easily the best of the Emeritus cycle here.
Pestbrood Sloth: Quietly excellent: 58.8% GIH, 5.8 IWD, and even OH/GD splits mean it pulls weight everywhere it shows up. Clean green common-ish stats transfer honestly. A premium green playable that goes earlier than ALSA 3.6 suggests — prioritize it.
Zimone's Experiment: 52% GIH, 35% play, and a 49% OH expose this as a do-nothing-early build-around. Wants a +1/+1 or ramp shell that SOS Quandrix doesn't reliably field. Niche sideboard/filler; skip unless your UG deck is deep on payoffs.
Colossus of the Blood Age: Strong gold uncommon — 58.5% GIH with a healthy GD 60% means it closes grindy Lorehold games as a colorless-bodied finisher. The artifact-creature top-end is exactly what RW value wants. Solid early pick, more reliable than its 'unmoved games' OH/GD gap suggests.
Growth Curve: Unplayable, full stop: 47.7% GIH, negative IWD, 12% play rate, DS 1.0. The field has correctly buried this UG ramp/draw spell as a non-card. Sideboard-only at absolute best; never main it.
Moment of Reckoning: A genuine WB bomb — 63.4% GIH and a massive 10.1 IWD, and crucially the WR is NOT just soup-inflation since the WWBB cost commits you hard to Silverquill. 'Turns any board into a win' is accurate. Premium first-pick, the WB payoff to draft around.
Quandrix Charm: Top-tier UG flexibility: 57.4% GIH, 6.1 IWD, and modal instant utility (pump-counter / scry / Naturalize) that's always live. Stats transfer cleanly. Strong early pick — the best Quandrix uncommon and a fine reason to lean UG.
Spectacular Skywhale: Believe the downgrade: 52% GIH and a 49% OH mean the 1/4 flyer gets blown out by the format's bounce far too often. The opus payoff rarely pays. Marginal UR filler — playable in a flyers-light deck but never a priority; let it wheel.
Vicious Rivalry: A rare sweeper in a format starved for them, and 58.8% GIH / 7.1 IWD prove it earns its slot — but it lives in Witherbloom, the worst archetype, which caps its ceiling. Strong pick if you're BG, otherwise the splash isn't worth it. Format-defining role, archetype-limited home.
Deathcap Glade: Premium fixing — 55.8% GIH with no OH/GD gap is exactly what you want from a dual land: it just enables your deck. Untapped BG fixing that supports splashes in the soup format. Solid early pick for fixing-hungry Converge decks; lands win this format.
Terramorphic Expanse: The 95% play rate tells the story — universal soup fixing and Lorehold graveyard fodder. LoL's caveat is right that the true 5c land outclasses it for Converge (one color + eats a basic), but at common this is premium, take-it-every-deck fixing.
Return to the Ranks: Damning: -3.5 IWD, 49% GIH, 0 OH (essentially never cast early), 22% play. A reanimation build-around with no support in SOS — your deck wins MORE without it. Sideboard/unplayable trap; the data is unambiguous.
Spell Pierce: Underrated tech, not a maindeck staple — 55% GIH with a low 42% play means the pilots who run it know when. LoL is right it punishes soup's expensive removal/payoffs cheaply. Maindeckable 1-2 copies in Prismari control; great sideboard card everywhere.
Vampiric Tutor: Stunningly bad in Limited: 46.5% GIH, -3.5 IWD, no OH/GD data, DS 1.7. Card disadvantage that finds one card you still have to cast — fine only if you tutor a true bomb. Generally a trap mythic; leave it for the rare cases your pool has a haymaker.
Return the Favor: Sharp UR soup tech with a 25% play rate but a strong 55% GD — it's a sideboard/situational blowout (copy their Wisdom of Ages, redirect removal) that's excellent when live and easy to pitch in red. Niche but powerful; great in the Prismari mirror, dead game-1.
Triumph of the Hordes: TRAP confirmed — no poison/blight support means it's a one-shot fog if it whiffs. 48.9% GIH, no OH/GD, DS 1.7. The infect payoff doesn't exist in this set. Unplayable; do not take, even in mono-green-go-wide.
Magus of the Library: The worst card here statistically: 45.5% GIH and a brutal -7.0 IWD — your deck is meaningfully better WITHOUT it. The card-draw engine needs an empty hand SOS green never achieves. Hard sideboard/cut; ignore the rarity, it's a non-card.
Rancorous Archaic: Filler 5c-soup body — the 53.6% GIH WR is propped up by Converge pilots casting it as a curve-topper. In a clean 2-color deck it's a vanilla last-card; only run it when short on playables, as Marshall did.
Ennis, Debate Moderator: 50% WR with negative IWD means it's doing nothing — a high play-rate trap that looks fine on curve but barely moves games. Sideboard-tier filler; fine as a body in Silverquill but don't pick it early.
Primary Research: Honest 54.9% from a cleanly-castable white enchantment — the near-zero IWD just means it's a steady engine, not a swing card. Solid playable role-player in any white deck, especially Repartee/Lorehold value.
Banishing Betrayal: Premium tempo common: 57.8% GD WR and +4.5 IWD on a cheap colorless-friendly bounce that surveils. The OH dip is normal for a reactive card. Buy-time spell every blue deck wants — strong early pick.
Fractal Anomaly: Hard pass — 48.4% with a 9% play rate and DS 1.0 is the data screaming sideboard. Even the modest IWD can't save a niche instant nobody mains. Unplayable outside a very specific build.
Orysa, Tide Choreographer: Mediocre legend — 53.4% leans on soup decks that can leverage the body, and LR's "bad but I love it" is honest. Playable filler in mono-blue/Converge, but don't overdraft on the legendary shine.
Arnyn, Deathbloom Botanist: Negative IWD and 50.6% mark this as a do-nothing rare — high play rate is a curve trap. Witherbloom is the worst archetype and this won't fix it; marginal filler at best.
Last Gasp: Premium removal, full stop — 57.4% GIH on a cleanly-castable 1B -3/-3 that transfers honestly to every deck. LR is right: there's no deck that doesn't want it. First-pick-quality interaction.
Scathing Shadelock: Below-curve body — 52.4% with negative IWD is filler that the high play rate flatters. Fine as a role-player in black decks but never an early pick; pure deck-fodder.
Charging Strifeknight: Experts are right to deflate it — 56.9% is decent but the haste-looter under-delivers versus its DS 3.4 hype. The 2-toughness is the SOS aggro trap. Solid playable, not the premium it looks like; take removal/value over it.
Maelstrom Artisan: Disappointing rare — 51.4% and flat IWD with a sub-40% play rate means most pilots can't make the Converge-style payoff sing. Soup-only ceiling; marginal in any focused deck.
Thunderdrum Soloist: Genuinely good red 3-drop — 55.2% with positive IWD and a 76% play rate, and it's cleanly mono-pip so the number transfers. Strong playable, a Lorehold/aggro curve filler that pulls its weight.
Emil, Vastlands Roamer: Honest 53% from a mono-green legend — solid ramp/fixing body that helps power the soup, modest but real. Good filler and a fine Converge enabler, not a high pick.
Planar Engineering: Underrated green rare — 56.0% with a chunky +6.5 IWD signals a real game-swinging effect that wins when it resolves. The 50% play rate suggests it's slept on; strong pickable, best in ramp/soup shells.
Abigale, Poet Laureate: Solid Silverquill payoff — 54.5% with identical OH/GD means consistent across game states, a reliable WB Repartee value engine. Strong playable in its colors, just gated by being gold; not a splash card.
Conciliator's Duelist: Premium WB bomb — 59.8% GIH and a 2.8 ALSA confirm it's worth the WWBB strain. The card draw + blink-on-Repartee engine is exactly what Silverquill wants. First-pick in the colors; commit to it.
Hardened Academic: Format-defining 2-drop — 60.8% GIH, DS 4.3, enabler-plus-payoff-plus-flying-haste-body for two mana. Cleanly RW so the number is real, and it's the Lorehold engine. Premium first-pick.
Nita, Forum Conciliator: Underwhelming WB rare — 51.4% with negative IWD and a sub-half play rate means it's not pulling weight even in its colors. Playable-ceiling filler; don't prioritize the legendary.
Quandrix, the Proof: The format's best card — 60.9% GIH, +11.5 IWD, 1.5 ALSA, 89% play. An Elder Dragon that wins games on resolution and anchors UG/soup. P1P1 over almost anything; bomb-tier.
Spirit Mascot: Quietly good RW common — 55.5% GIH and a strong OH WR show the graveyard-counter enabler earns its slot early. LR's arc to C+ undersold it; solid Lorehold playable that curves and synergizes.
Visionary's Dance: LoL nails it — 56.3% and +5.7 IWD that rises with rebuy (Wisdom of Ages) make this a clunky over-performer. Prismari control's bread-and-butter; strong pickable, and prioritize it higher once you have the recursion.
Dreamroot Cascade: Excellent fixing land — 56.5% with +5.4 IWD because it enables the Converge/soup plan with zero card disadvantage. Cleanly playable in any UG-touching deck; high-priority pick that quietly wins the mana war.
Titan's Grave: Premium common fixing — 55.3% and high play rate from a land that just makes the soup work; the value is real and color-agnostic. Take dual lands like this early in a Converge format.
Winds of Abandon: Bomb white mythic — 61.7% GIH with a massive 65% GD WR, a one-sided board wipe on the overload mode. The OH/GD split is normal for a sweeper. P1P1-quality; build around it.
Stock Up: The card-draw benchmark — 59.8% GIH and +8.7 IWD on a cleanly-castable blue sorcery that transfers to every deck. LR's right that nothing else clears this bar. Premium, near-bomb pick.
Zombify: Unplayable — 46.4% with a 13% play rate and DS 1.0; reanimation has no payoff density in SOS. Sideboard-only even in black; pass it every time.
Subterranean Tremors: Red mythic bomb — 60.5% GIH and +9.0 IWD on a scalable sweeper-plus-finisher. Wins soup mirrors and grindy games on resolution. P1P1-tier; first-pick over nearly everything.
Veil of Summer: No 17Lands signal (zero WR, 8% play) — it's a narrow sideboard hate card against blue/black, useless maindeck in Limited. Sideboard-only; never a pick priority.
Sylvan Library: Surprisingly flat — 50.7% and negative IWD show this Constructed staple is a trap in Limited where the life payments hurt and the card selection underwhelms. The DS 3.0 hype overrates it; marginal mythic, often a sideboard card.
Sundering Archaic: Premium colorless uncommon and a true any-deck snap-pick: 58.8 WR with a clean colorless cost means it transfers honestly, not soup-juiced. Splashable disruption that hits artifacts/enchantments and exiles graveyards in spell mirrors. Take early in any seat.
Erode: Premium white removal and a strong early pick. Path-to-Exile-on-a-creature/planeswalker with negligible downside; the 56.2 WR is honest mono-pip white that holds up in any deck. The low ramp-mode IWD just reflects how rarely the land matters. First-pickable.
Quill-Blade Laureate: Strong white playable with honest stats (mono-pip, IWD near zero is fine for a repeatable value creature). Best in Silverquill as a Repartee enabler/sandbag finisher; happily run multiples. Numot's 'whatever in Lorehold' is fair — it wants the WB aggro home.
Brush Off: Soup-conditioned: 60.6 GD WR is inflated by spell-dense Prismari/soup mirrors where it's a premium tempo counter. The 5.1 ALSA and 74% play rate confirm it's a payoff, not a maindeck staple. Strong in UR control, mediocre in proactive 2-color decks.
Fractalize: Marginal/sideboard filler. 51.5 WR, 7.1 ALSA, and 18.7% play rate all scream niche combat trick that rarely makes the cut. Only a Quandrix consideration when you need the instant-speed tempo; pass otherwise.
Pensive Professor: Premium blue rare — and the stats transfer cleanly. 58.7 WR, elite 8.5 IWD, 2.1 ALSA, near-90% play rate make it a first-pick anywhere blue. Mono-pip card advantage engine that's good in control and soup alike, no inflation caveat.
Burrog Banemaker: Solid filler at best — 54.5 WR on a black common with 5.9 ALSA and flat IWD marks it a replaceable body. Fine roleplayer in Silverquill/black-based decks but never a priority; take it late to round out the curve.
Lecturing Scornmage: Strong black uncommon with a notable split: 60.9 OH WR vs 55.9 GD says it front-loads impact (proactive ETB/tempo) and fades late. Best in aggressive Silverquill where you want to be ahead early. Solid early pick, honest mono-pip stats.
Scheming Silvertongue: Premium black rare and a near-first-pick: 59.0 WR, 1.9 ALSA, 88% play rate. Strong OH WR (60.5) means it pressures early. Clean black home in any B deck — no soup inflation, this one's just good.
Choreographed Sparks: Unplayable. 44.0 WR, negative IWD, 10% play rate — the data and LR's 'F' fully agree. A red rare trap that does nothing for the board. Hard pass, not even sideboard.
Magmablood Archaic: Strong colorless-adjacent rare with honest stats — 56.5 WR, 2.4 ALSA, 86% play rate. The GD>OH split says it's a value/midrange threat that's better the longer the game goes. Reliable early pick in any red deck or red-leaning soup.
Tome Blast: Premium common — Firebolt with flashback is a 2-for-1 burn engine. 57.3 WR and 90% play rate confirm it transfers everywhere. As LoL notes, it punishes flimsy aggro creatures, so size yours accordingly. Take highly; it's never a wrong pick.
Environmental Scientist: The best card in this batch and a premium early pick (4.2 DS, 61.0 WR, elite 9.1 IWD). It's a 2/2 that fixes for Converge — the single most valuable thing in a soup format. Snap-pick when mana is shaky, which is always. LR's 'somehow free' is right.
Shopkeeper's Bane: Marginal green common — 53.2 WR, 6.6 ALSA, sub-50% play rate. A late-table body/artifact-hate roleplayer, not a maindeck staple. Take to fill curve or sideboard against Trove/artifact decks; otherwise leave it.
Abstract Paintmage: Filler/marginal Prismari two-color — 50.2 WR and 48.9% play rate mark it as a UR scrap creature exactly as the format doctrine predicts (those small bodies don't pay off). Playable only if you're light on UR cards; never a priority.
Cuboid Colony: Strong Quandrix two-drop despite Numot's lukewarm take — 56.1 WR and 5.0 IWD back LR's enthusiasm over the 'okay 2-drop' reads. Flash/flying/trample with counters enables tempo and scaling. Solid early pick in UG; the data sides with LR here.
Imperious Inkmage: Solid WB common — flat IWD and 6.6 ALSA say it's a replaceable Repartee body, but 58.1 OH WR shows it's a fine aggressive two-drop-plus. Good filler in Silverquill aggro; agrees with LSV's C+. Take mid-pack, don't reach.
Old-Growth Educator: Filler in the format's worst archetype (Witherbloom). 53.9 WR, flat IWD, 4.7 ALSA — a fine body that can't lift BG out of last place. Playable roleplayer if you're forced into Witherbloom; otherwise a sign to be in a different color pair.
Rapturous Moment: Soup/UR-conditioned filler — 55.8 GD vs 49.3 OH WR is a stark split flagging a do-nothing-early card that needs spell density to pay off. 48% play rate and 5.7 ALSA confirm role-player status. Prismari control only; mediocre elsewhere.
Splatter Technique: Soup bomb — the gaudy 62.7 GD WR and 11.1 IWD are partly inflated by 5c pilots casting it as a mass-removal-plus-draw-4 haymaker. Still elite in any UR/soup shell (86% play rate, 2.3 ALSA). Just temper expectations in a clean 2-color UR build.
Wilt in the Heat: Strong Lorehold removal — 5 damage with exile, cost-reduced by graveyard activity. 56.9 WR is honest for a WR build-around; the GD>OH split shows it's a value-game card. Premium in RW graveyard decks, merely playable outside them. Solid early pick in Lorehold.
Fields of Strife: Strong colorless utility land — 57.0 WR with even OH/GD splits and 79% play rate means free value any deck happily runs. Goes a bit late (5.5 ALSA) so you can table it. Snag it as a low-opportunity-cost include, especially in aggressive decks.
Akroma's Will: Premium white mythic and a bomb finisher — 62.7 GD WR, 89% play rate. The flexible anthem/protection package wins races and games outright. Honest white stats that transfer to any W deck; first-pick caliber. Practiced-Offense-tier game-ender.
Brain Freeze: Hard soup/UR-mirror conditioned: the 8.7-point OH-to-GD gap (49.5 vs 58.7) is the biggest tell in the batch — dead in your opener, lethal in a spell-stuffed late game. LR's 'live in fear of it' is mirror-specific. Premium in UR control, near-unplayable in 2-color aggro.
Ad Nauseam: No data (0 WR, 6.8% play rate, 1.7 DS) — effectively unplayable in this Limited format. A black mythic with no Limited home; the life-loss draw engine is a constructed card. Sideboard/hate-pick only.
Abrade: Premium flexible removal — kill a creature or blow up an artifact, and Trove/Loot/Arc make the artifact mode live constantly. 58.1 WR, 93% play rate, 2.9 ALSA: an honest mono-red staple that transfers to every red deck. Snap-pick early.
Awaken the Woods: Soup-leaning green mythic — strong 6.0 IWD but a soft 54.0 WR and the OH<GD split (50.9 vs 55.8) show it does nothing early and pays off as ramp/fixing into bombs. Best in 5c green-base soup; playable but not the slam its rarity suggests.
Bring to Light: Soup-only payoff — 26.7% play rate confirms it's the toolbox tutor for greedy 5c decks where Converge=5 fetches your best card. 53.1 WR is unimpressive because casual pilots misuse it. Niche soup gem; a wasted pick in any focused 2-3 color deck.
Adrix and Nev, Twincasters: Underwhelming UG mythic — 48.3 WR and 38% play rate are damning for a 1.7 DS. The token-doubling payoff is too narrow for this format and it's a do-nothing legend if you lack token synergy. Speculative at best; usually a cut despite the rarity.
Together as One: The format's best card, and unlike most Converge payoffs this one doesn't NEED full soup to win — even at X=4 in a 3-color deck it's a one-card Cruel Ultimatum. Premium first-pick, slam every time.
Graduation Day: 50.8% WR and negative IWD confirm the experts: a build-around enchantment that does nothing without a recursion engine you won't reliably have. Sideboard/unplayable in nearly every deck.
Rapier Wit: Combat trick with a 54.3% WR but late ALSA (7.0) and low play rate — fine but unexciting. Solid filler combat trick for Silverquill/Lorehold aggro; not a card you prioritize.
Campus Composer: The classic blue trap: looks like a value engine, plays like filler. 53.9% WR and the experts' downgrade confirm it — playable role-player in a controlling blue deck but never a card you're happy to first-pick.
Harmonized Trio: A rare that's really a 1/2 attached to a Brainstorm — 53.3% WR, dies for free, card-disadvantage if removed. Mediocre filler value piece; take it for blue but don't overrate the rarity.
Procrastinate: Quietly one of blue's better pseudo-removal commons — locks a creature down for several turns. 52.3% WR undersells it as repeatable late-game removal; a fine playable in controlling blue decks, prioritize when light on answers.
Cheerful Osteomancer: Black's deck wanted answers and went long, not a creature curve, so this underperformed to 51.4% and negative IWD. Marginal filler — playable body in an aggressive Silverquill build, cuttable in soup.
Leech Collector: 65.8% play rate but only 51.8% WR with negative IWD — a high-floor body that doesn't actually move games. Solid filler creature for black decks; nothing to prioritize.
Send in the Pest: Experts split, but the 55.1% WR and even OH/GD splits side with LR over LoL — the discard-plus-body is genuinely fine Witherbloom/aggro fuel. Solid role-player despite LoL's pan; better than its reputation.
Duel Tactics: Niche red fight/tempo sorcery with a respectable 54.6% WR but low 36.9% play rate — it's a build-dependent answer. Solid filler removal when you have the creatures to enable it; otherwise sideboard.
Mica, Reader of Ruins: 52.9% WR and a 29% play rate flag a build-around legend that most decks can't support. Role-player at best; only worth it when your red-base deck actually has the artifact/spell density to enable it.
Unsubtle Mockery: The premium red removal common — 57.5% WR, 92.9% play rate, and the spell LSV reached for first. Cleanly castable so the WR transfers honestly; strong playable removal, prioritize in any red deck.
Follow the Lumarets: Lowest play rate (25.7%) and a sub-water IWD — a green build-around token sorcery that rarely earns its slot. Sideboard/marginal; only consider in a dedicated go-wide green deck.
Slumbering Trudge: 59% WR but the WR is honest (clean XG cast) — a midrange beater that overruns ground stalls. Note the archetype mismatch: great in green beatdown, awkward in Prismari/soup, and don't splash for it. Strong in its home.
Applied Geometry: 57.9% WR with a huge 7.9 IWD — a Simic value bomb that genuinely swings games. WR is somewhat soup/value-deck conditioned, but the IWD is real; strong playable and a premium pick for any UG/soup deck.
Dina's Guidance: 45.1% WR, negative IWD, 7.8% play rate, 0 OH — this is a near-unplayable BG build-around in the format's worst color pair. Hard sideboard; the rarity is a trap.
Inkling Mascot: 56.2% WR looks good but the experts are right to flag it as a generous C — a vanilla-ish Repartee flyer that often gets cut from Silverquill. Honest WR (mono-pip-ish), so a fine filler aggro body, not a payoff.
Paradox Surveyor: 58.6% WR with 6.7 IWD on an uncommon — a UG value creature that pulls real weight. WR is mildly value-deck-inflated but the IWD confirms it's the real deal; strong playable, prioritize in Simic/soup.
Render Speechless: The hidden gem — best non-rare answer to soup, splashable even into Prismari. LoL's biggest mover-up and LR went D+ to splash-worthy; the 57.4% WR (clean cast) backs them. Strong playable, take it higher than ALSA suggests.
Stadium Tidalmage: 54% WR but a low 7.1 ALSA and 39% play rate — a fringe Prismari spells-matter body. Solid filler in a dedicated UR control shell; replaceable and easy to cut elsewhere.
Witherbloom Charm: Flexible BG instant but the 53.8% WR and the 'fine, not exciting' read are dead-on — the MV2 kill clause is too narrow. Decent filler removal/flex in BG; nothing to reach for in the worst archetype.
Forum of Amity: A fixing land with a 55.9% WR and 72% play rate — basically free value for the soup/Converge plans this format rewards. Strong include; near-auto-play in any multicolor deck for the fixing alone.
Angel's Grace: No meaningful data (0 WR, 5% play rate, 0.9 DS) — a corner-case Fog-style instant with no Limited home. Unplayable/sideboard.
Cyclonic Rift: 58.4% WR, 1.9 ALSA, 61.1% GD WR — a premier blue bomb even on the cheap mode (overload is a near-unbeatable blowout). Honest blue card; premium first-pick, slam it.
Bitter Triumph: Premium cheap black removal — 57.5% WR, 2.8 ALSA, 90.8% play rate, kills nearly anything for two mana. Cleanly castable so the WR transfers; strong first-pick-quality removal in any black deck.
Big Score: Treasure-plus-card draw that smooths and fixes — 55.1% WR and a strong-deck enabler for soup/Prismari ramp. WR is partly soup-conditioned but the fixing matters; strong playable, especially in greedy multicolor decks.
Berserk: No WR data and a 24% play rate — a swingy mythic combat trick that can blow out or whiff. Niche finisher in green aggro; speculative role-player, not a reliable include.
Culling Ritual: 52.7% WR but a notable 5.6 IWD — a BG sweeper-plus-ramp that overperforms when it lands. Build-dependent (needs cheap targets); a solid splashable answer-sweeper for soup, weaker in tight 2-color.
Codie, Vociferous Codex: A spells-only build-around legend — 54% WR and 35% play rate show it works in dedicated zero-creature shells but is a non-bo elsewhere. Niche payoff; only pick if you're committing to the spells-matter plan.
Transcendent Archaic: 56.9% GIH is soup-inflated — its body scales with colors converged, so that WR reflects 4-5c casts and craters in a clean 2-color deck. Solid Converge finisher, mid-pick payoff, not a proactive 2-color playable.
Group Project: 57% GIH with an 82% play rate and -0.5 IWD reads as honest filler that's nearly always maindeckable, not a bomb. Best in go-wide Lorehold where the scaling pays off; solid mid-pick role-player.
Rehearsed Debater: Cleanly-castable white common, so 54.7% transfers honestly — that's filler, not a 49% play rate worth chasing. Fine body in Silverquill/Lorehold curves but late-pick, replaceable.
Chase Inspiration: 21.9% play rate and 7.9 ALSA say it's a niche role-player that wheels easily; the 56.2% GD WR comes from the few decks that actually want it. Strong IWD when drawn — a fine reactive blue value card, but low priority.
Homesickness: 55.8% with 5.3 IWD on a 71% play-rate blue tempo instant — reads as a genuinely good, cleanly-castable bounce/tempo tool. Strong playable in any blue deck, especially spell-dense Prismari control; solid early-mid pick.
Quick Study: Mono-pip blue common with honest 56.1% GIH and 5.1 IWD — that's a strong, splash-proof card-draw playable, not the filler a 2.7 DS implies. High-floor pickup for any blue deck.
Cost of Brilliance: 57.3% transfers cleanly (single-pip-friendly) and LoL nails it — this is an aggressive-black engine, weak in soup where better draw exists. Premium in Silverquill/Witherbloom midrange; draw-plus-counter keeps proactive decks fueled.
Masterful Flourish: 56% GIH but a 34% play rate and 0.4 IWD flag a situational combat trick that's only good in the right black tempo deck. Solid filler when you're proactive, sideboard-flexible otherwise.
Sneering Shadewriter: 52.7% with negative IWD on a cleanly-castable common — honest mediocrity, a filler body that doesn't earn its slot. Late pick at best, first to cut in a deep black pool.
Emeritus of Conflict: 2.2 ALSA and 87% play rate confirm the LR A-: a 1R 2/2 first striker that hands you a Bolt once you've cast three spells. Premium aggressive red two-drop — easy early pick in Lorehold/Prismari, fuels the spell count too.
Molten-Core Maestro: 59.6% GIH with 1.9 ALSA, 93% play, +6.9 IWD — this is a genuine bomb that goes first. Honestly castable in mono-red, format-warping in any spells deck; premium first-pick, no soup asterisk.
Zealous Lorecaster: 54.7% with +2.2 IWD reads as a solid spells-payoff common; the cute infinite loops are upside, not the floor. Good in Prismari/Lorehold where spells flow — strong mid-pick role-player, replaceable.
Germination Practicum: 58.4% GIH, 1.6 ALSA, +8 IWD — a premium green Lesson bomb that's a real P1 even though green is the soup-enabling color. The green paradigm engine; take it highly, it carries.
Snarl Song: 61.7% GIH and a monster +11.2 IWD, but heavily soup-conditioned — that WR is X=4-5 casts and the single green pip makes it the format's signature Converge magnet. Premium build-around / P1P1 that commits you to soup; mediocre as a clean 5G two-token spell.
Ark of Hunger: 63.3% GIH, 2.0 ALSA, 90% play — the data backs LSV's nightmare: a Lorehold graveyard-value engine that grinds you out. Premium first-pick in RW, and the WR is honest since it's a fixed 2RW artifact, not soup-scaled.
Elemental Mascot: LR's downgrade is correct — 54.5% on a 1/4 flier whose UR-aggro home never existed. The 55.6% GD WR is generous; it's a defensible filler blocker with an opus tag, not a card to prioritize.
Killian's Confidence: Top Silverquill common and the honest WB casting cost means 56.6% transfers — pump-plus-draw that recurs is exactly the WB Repartee/aggro engine. Strong playable, high pick in Silverquill, the gas-keeper Cost of Brilliance wishes it could splash.
Pest Mascot: 53.2% with negative IWD on a 73% play rate — a high-floor-but-low-ceiling BG body, and BG is the format's worst archetype. Filler that fills the deck; play it in Witherbloom but it won't win games on its own.
Resonating Lute: 56.1% GIH undersells it — LR's 'Mana Flare + Library' rating shows this is an A in spell-dense Prismari, and the modest WR reflects how many marginal decks ran it. Premium in UR control, a trap in creature decks; archetype-conditional bomb.
Startled Relic Sloth: 57.3% with 3.9 ALSA, 83% play — a cleanly-rated WR uncommon that's just a good Lorehold body with relic upside. Strong playable, solid mid-pick; honest stats, no soup inflation.
Witherbloom, the Balancer: 50.7% GIH with sub-50 OH on a mythic dragon is damning — BG is the worst archetype and this five-drop doesn't fix it. The 75% play rate is people forcing a legendary bomb that underperforms; sideboard-tier, low priority.
Great Hall of the Biblioplex: 58.7% GIH but soup-conditioned — its value is keeping every Converge color live, so the WR reflects 5c pilots. Premium fixing-land in soup (take over Terramorphic), but a near-blank in a focused 2-color deck.
Armageddon: 51.3% GIH, -2.5 IWD, zero OH WR on record — a symmetric Limited trap that whiffs without a board-ahead setup. Sideboard/build-around only; the white go-wide deck rarely assembles the lead to make it a bomb.
Daze: 58.2% with +6.4 IWD, but LR's caveat is the whole story — premium only on the play in a heavy-Island deck, mediocre otherwise. Strong in dedicated Prismari/mono-blue tempo; situational rare, not a universal first-pick.
Culling the Weak: 4.4% play rate, no meaningful WR data — a niche ritual with no payoff shell in this format. Sideboard/unplayable; skip it.
Brotherhood's End: 59.7% GIH, +7.9 IWD, 78% play — a premium flexible red sweeper that's honestly castable (RR-ish, not soup-scaled). Modal board control hits go-wide and artifacts; high first-pick in any red deck.
Crop Rotation: 5.7% play rate and 0.9 DS — no land-toolbox payoff exists in SOS, so it's a dead card. Unplayable; leave it in the pack.
Deflecting Palm: 47.1% GIH and 17.5% play rate — a narrow reactive trick that needs a specific incoming hit to matter. Sideboard tech against big creatures/aggro at best; maindeck-unplayable.
Library of Alexandria: LoL's 'WASHED' call is right and the splits prove it — 67.9% OH WR (great in a fast opener) but only 55.1% GD as a midgame draw, because soup goes way over the top. Strong in low-curve aggressive decks, overrated in the grindy soup that the format rewards; good, not the windmill-slam its name suggests.
Ajani's Response: Honest 57.6% — clean mono-W removal that transfers fully, no soup inflation. The tapped-creature discount makes it a tempo-positive blowout in attacking matchups. Strong playable interaction; take it over filler, just not over premium bombs.
Harsh Annotation: Dead-flat 50% with negative IWD and 25% play rate — the data says it's barely deck-worthy. Conditional/narrow effect that the field correctly leaves in the board. Sideboard/last-cuts only.
Restoration Seminar: The 57.6% / 4.8 IWD / 2.1 ALSA is real and goes early, but LR's skepticism on setup cost is fair — it's a value reanimation Lesson, not a tempo play. Premium for grindy W decks and soup; less impressive in fast Silverquill.
Deluge Virtuoso: 52% and a 6.6 ALSA confirm the experts: the UR prowess-aggro dream died, so this is a trap that locks you into blue for a marginal payoff. Only playable with a real prowess/spell density (Firedancer, Iteration). Filler-to-fringe otherwise.
Hydro-Channeler: Cleanly-cast mono-U body with a 54.7%/3.1 IWD that transfers honestly — no soup inflation here. Reliable spell-velocity role-player for any blue deck, especially Prismari control. Solid playable, not a high pick.
Run Behind: GD WR 59.1% looks great but that's soup/Converge-adjacent value tempo — the bounce-with-upside shines in flexible decks. LoL's right that cheaper Banishing Betrayal is often better. Strong filler interaction; good vs. fractals, fine in blue.
Decorum Dissertation: 56.1% with a 2.0 ALSA, but this paradigm is genuinely the weakest — the lose-2 clause backfires against lifegain and you're not obligated to recast it. Soup/control payoff that's a build-around, not a slam. Good but overdrafted; take the cleaner cards.
Melancholic Poet: 56% with negative IWD and 56% OH — a fine curve-filling body whose value is front-loaded, not game-swinging. Honest mono-B common that plays in any black deck. Solid role-player, unexciting middle pick.
Tragedy Feaster: A 7/6 ward for 2BB at 55.1% — powerful when active but the sac clause makes it a Witherbloom-only liability you can misplay into a blowout. Black's worst archetype is its home, which caps the upside. Playable rare, handle with care.
Expressive Firedancer: 56.2% OH and grows out of burn range, but LR's C- and the dead UR-aggro deck mean the 6.2 ALSA is real — nobody's building around it. A fine prowess body that combos with Deluge Virtuoso if you're already deep blue-red. Filler payoff.
Pigment Wrangler: 51% with negative IWD despite a 69% play rate — players keep running it and it keeps underperforming. The 4.5 ALSA overrates a body that doesn't move win-rate. Marginal filler; cut for anything proactive.
Aberrant Manawurm: 50.8% / 37% play — a green uncommon the field largely benches. The downside outweighs the stats and it doesn't fit the soup or the proactive 2-color decks. Sideboard/marginal.
Glorious Decay: 6.4% play rate and a 8.2 ALSA say nearly nobody plays it; the 51.5% is noise off a tiny sample. Narrow sweeper-style effect that rarely lines up. Sideboard tech at best.
Studious First-Year: The best green common, full stop — 56.9% with a format-leading 5.4 IWD and 59.4% OH, all cleanly mono-G so it transfers everywhere. A 1-drop that ramps and fixes for the soup. Premium early pick, snap it.
Aziza, Mage Tower Captain: 53% with negative IWD undersells LR's B+ ceiling — the copy-a-spell threat warps games but needs a creature-and-spell board to fire, which Lorehold doesn't always assemble. Build-around RW rare; powerful but inconsistent. Strong in the right deck.
Embrace the Paradox: 56.3% GD WR is soup-inflated — that 5.2 IWD reflects flexible UG/Converge shells getting full value. In a clean 2-color deck it's a worse trick. Strong in soup, ordinary playable elsewhere.
Kirol, History Buff: 55.7% with negative IWD but a 56.4% OH — a proactive Lorehold/RW 2-drop whose value is best on curve, not topdecked. Honestly castable gold uncommon that fits the real aggressive shell. Solid playable.
Practiced Scrollsmith: The 59% / 60.7% GD is the highest in this batch and it's a genuine Lorehold hallmark, not soup noise — a 3/2 first strike that exiles a graveyard card is pure proactive value. LR nails it: if this is bad the deck doesn't work. Premium, take early.
Root Manipulation: 52.7% understates it — LoL is right this is an underrated overrun where menace beats trample given the format's low creature counts. Witherbloom's best top-end that steals games the archetype otherwise can't win. Sleeper high pick in BG.
Stirring Honormancer: 58% with a strong 59% GD and clean castability — a premium Silverquill 5-drop that grinds card advantage for the WB midrange/aggro deck. The data and both experts align. Strong playable, near-bomb in its home.
Zaffai and the Tempests: 51.7% overall with a 46.6% OH is a red flag — that 54.4% GD is soup pilots salvaging it, not a real signal. The UR shell it wants doesn't aggro and it's mediocre topdecked. Fringe playable; don't prioritize.
Paradox Gardens: 56.2% with a 4.2 IWD — but it's a UG dual land, so the win-rate is just good fixing enabling the soup, not card power. Free inclusion that smooths Converge; take it as a fixer when on plan. Strong support pick, not a payoff.
Duty Beyond Death: 50.8% with negative IWD and a 16% play rate — a narrow combat trick the field rightly leaves out. Doesn't impact the board enough. Sideboard only.
Deduce: 57.4% with a stellar 5.8 IWD, cleanly mono-U — instant-speed draw a card or make a clue, all honest, no soup inflation. Flexible card advantage that any blue deck wants. Strong playable, takes early in blue.
Dismember: 58.8% with a 1.8 ALSA and 96.8% play rate — near-universal premium removal that's effectively colorless (Phyrexian mana), so it transfers to every deck and the soup. The format's cleanest removal first-pick. Slam it.
Bulk Up: 51% with a 19% play rate — a double-strike trick that only earns its slot with heavy prowess, which barely exists here. Even Deluge Virtuoso can't reliably make it worth it. Sideboard/build-around only.
Giant Growth: 51.6% / 20% play — a vanilla pump spell that doesn't move the needle in a format that wants permanents and value. Two-for-one risk with little upside. Marginal filler at best.
Expressive Iteration: 57.5% with the batch's best 6.7 IWD and a 3.2 ALSA — premium Prismari card advantage that's the real UR control payoff (not the dead aggro plan). Mostly honest since UR is a real shell. Strong first-pick-quality in blue-red.
Antiquities on the Loose: Premium first-pick, full stop — 64% GIH WR on a 90% play rate and ALSA under 2 is honest, not soup-inflated, because two 2/2s for 1WW plus a flashback that returns them bigger is castable in any white deck. Best army-in-a-can in the format; bomb-tier.
Honorbound Page: Solid filler for proactive white (Silverquill/Lorehold). 53% WR with negative IWD says it's a body, not a payoff — the first-strike + prepared flying mode makes it a fine early curve-filler that goes very late (ALSA 6.5), so prioritize when you need 2-drops, not earlier.
Shattered Acolyte: Strong playable for a common and the cleanest kind of stats — mono-pip 1W lifelink 2/2 with maindeckable artifact/enchantment hate. 56% WR transfers honestly to any white deck; the sac mode is upside against a format full of Potioner's Trove and equipment. Snap it up around pick 5-6.
Divergent Equation: Soup-mirror bomb, and the split tells the story: 51.6% OH but 61.3% GD WR — this is a topdeck/late-game XXU engine that's only premium when you're the 4-5c control deck recurring it. In a clean 2-color shell it's a clunky win-more; draft it high only if you're committed to soup.
Jadzi, Steward of Fate: Premium first-pick bomb. 59% GIH at ALSA 1.9 with 90% play — a flexible blue engine that goes early because everyone wants it. Honest stats; take it P1P1 and let it pull you toward a controlling, spell-heavy build.
Skycoach Conductor: Strong playable bordering on bomb for blue. A 2U 2/3 flier that free-blinks your own creature every combat is a repeatable value/protection engine — 56.8% WR, ALSA 2.5. Best in blink-y soup or any deck with ETB creatures; take it early.
Dissection Practice: Strong playable removal/trick for black. 58% GIH with tight OH/GD spread (57.5/58.3) means it performs consistently regardless of archetype — a maindeck instant that earns its slot. Reliable pick around 3-4; not a bomb but always good.
Moseo, Vein's New Dean: Premium black bomb — 60% GIH, ALSA 2.0, 88% play. Honest mono-color stats that transfer to any black deck. Take it near the top of the pack; a rare that anchors Witherbloom/Silverquill alike despite Witherbloom being the worst pair.
Ulna Alley Shopkeep: Sideboard/unplayable. Sub-50% WR, 13.6% play rate, ALSA 7.8, DS 1.0 — the data and the draft community agree this is a last-pick filler creature. Only run it if your black deck is desperately short on bodies.
Flashback: Trap rare — the OH/GD split is brutal: 48.5% on the play vs 57.5% drawn, meaning it's dead early and only shines as a reactive late-game answer. Net 54% is mediocre for a rare. Playable role-player in spell-dense UR control, not a high pick.
Rearing Embermare: Sideboard/unplayable. Sub-50% WR, 9.4% play, ALSA 8.1 — a vanilla-ish red common that doesn't make the cut in a format where 2-toughness aggro creatures are scrap. Leave it in the pile.
Additive Evolution: Strong playable green enchantment, and the gap (55.2 OH / 58.3 GD) suggests a value engine that rewards a built-around board. 57% WR at ALSA 3.6 is real — a fine green payoff, best in a creature-heavy or +1/+1 shell. Solid mid-pick.
Hungry Graffalon: Marginal filler at best. 50.7% WR but only 15% play and ALSA 7.8 — a green common body that's last-pick fodder; the slightly-positive IWD is noise from desperate decks. Take only to fill a green curve.
Tenured Concocter: Solid filler / role-player. 52% WR, ALSA 7.2 — the experts are right that a big troll body bullies the board and enables Witherbloom lifegain, but the modest numbers cap it as a playable late-pick, not a priority. Good in green-based decks short on size.
Berta, Wise Extrapolator: Strong playable Simic rare — but the 53.2 OH / 56.5 GD split flags it as a grindy value engine that wants the long game, fitting the soup/control lean of UG. 55% WR, ALSA 2.3; take it early in a spell/value deck, less exciting in aggro.
Essenceknit Scholar: Strong playable, and surprisingly honest given BG is the format's worst pair — 58% GIH with a balanced split (58.6/57.7) means this uncommon Warlock pulls its own weight regardless. A real reason to consider Witherbloom; high pick in that lane.
Lluwen, Exchange Student: Strong playable BG legend, though the 53.6 OH / 57.5 GD gap shows it's a value creature that wants to grind. 56% WR is good for the format's worst archetype — take it to anchor a Witherbloom build, but don't force the colors for it.
Prismari Charm: Strong playable for UR control — flexible instant (damage/bounce/draw) at 56.7% with a 54/58.3 split, slightly drawn-better as expected of a reactive card. Experts rate it just behind Silverquill's charm; agree. Core role-player in the best 2-color shell.
Sanar, Unfinished Genius: Premium UR uncommon — 58.8% GIH, strong 7.6 IWD, and unusually a HIGHER OH WR (59.4) meaning it's proactive enough to win on the play. Excellent in Prismari spell-control; take it early, it's one of the better UR cards in the set.
Stress Dream: Premium and underrated, exactly as the experts say — 5 damage AND a card-selection mode at instant speed is removal that replaces itself. 60% GIH, 89% play; the 56.4/61.9 split shows it's even better in the long game. High pick in any U/R deck; don't pass it.
Biblioplex Tomekeeper: Unplayable — the worst card in the set per the experts and the data backs it: 45.5% GIH, 40% OH, 2.2% play, ALSA 8.7. A colorless artifact whose teased synergy never materialized. Never maindeck.
Petrified Hamlet: No 17Lands data (0% WR, 1.4% play) — a rare utility land that virtually nobody plays. Treat as fringe/sideboard; a fixing-light land that doesn't justify a slot in a Converge format where you want real dual-producing fixing instead.
Helping Hand: Marginal filler. 53% WR but negative IWD and only 46% play — a cheap white reanimation/recursion sorcery that's only as good as your best creature to return, situational. Playable role-player in grindy white decks; not a priority pick.
Disdainful Stroke: Soup-mirror specialist — the 53.2 OH / 59.0 GD split is the tell: a late-game/mirror over-performer (Mystical Archive) that's a dead card against aggro. 56.9% overall is inflated by control pilots; premium in the 5c soup mirror, a sideboard liability otherwise. Value it by your matchup, not the raw WR.
Feed the Swarm: Strong playable, premium-ish unconditional removal — kills any creature OR enchantment for 1B at sorcery speed, ALSA 3.5. The 55% WR slightly undersells it because the life payment hurts some decks; in practice it's flexible answer-everything removal. High black pick.
Burst Lightning: Premium red removal — 93% play, ALSA 2.8, 58.7% honest mono-pip stats that transfer to any red deck. Cheap, scalable, instant-speed; take it near the top of any pack. One of the best commons/uncommons for proactive red decks.
Glimpse of Nature: No meaningful data (0% WR, 4% play) — a build-around green card-draw rare that needs a dedicated creature-flood deck SOS doesn't really support. Niche/unplayable in most pools; only consider in a hyper-dense green go-wide shell.
Fracture: No 17Lands WR data but it's premium WB removal on sight — instant-speed destroy artifact/creature/enchantment for WB. The 10% play just reflects how few decks hit both pips; in a Silverquill or soup deck it's flexible, clean removal. High pick when castable.
Ascendant Dustspeaker: The experts are right to bump it: a 55.3% WR on a cleanly-castable white common with negative IWD means it's a sticky body that shines in the slow soup mirrors where graveyard hate matters. Solid filler-plus, takes any deck, higher floor than ceiling.
Informed Inkwright: Premium first-pick. 1.8 ALSA and +6.2 IWD on a mono-W rare means this WR transfers honestly to any white deck — it's not soup-inflated. A 2-drop that snowballs Repartee into a flier army; Silverquill/Lorehold's dream, and good even splashed.
Soaring Stoneglider: Filler. The 53.7% GIH with negative IWD says it's a body you play but never want, and the OH/GD gap means it's a clunky topdeck. Fine in any white deck as a curve-filler, but never a reason to be in white.
Echocasting Symposium: The +8.9 IWD is the tell — this Lesson is a recurring engine that buries grindy soup/Prismari control games, exactly where blue wants to be. But the low OH WR (53.7) confirms it does nothing early; soup-mirror payoff, not a tempo play. Strong pick in the right shell.
Landscape Painter: Solid filler with a soup tilt — the positive IWD and gd_wr over oh_wr say it's a midrange body that helps you Converge/fix into 5c, not a tempo card. Playable in any blue deck, best when you're fixing toward soup.
Spellbook Seeker: Marginal. Sub-51 GIH and a 27.5% play rate is the format telling you this is a 23rd-card filler at best. Only makes the cut in spell-heavy Prismari that needs the body; otherwise sideboard.
Emeritus of Woe: Premium bomb, full stop — 1.5 ALSA and a clean mono-B pip mean the 59.1% WR is honest, not soup-juiced. A 5/4 that hands you Demonic Tutor is a first-pick in any black deck; the experts undersell nothing here.
Poisoner's Apprentice: Strong playable. 55.2% on a cleanly-castable black uncommon with a 4.3 ALSA — this transfers to any black deck, no soup discount. A proactive body that fits Witherbloom's grind or Silverquill's tempo; good value-per-pip.
Wander Off: The experts undersell this — 55.8% GIH at common with an 87% play rate makes it a top-tier removal staple, and the exile clause is gold in a graveyard-value format full of flashback/recursion. Premium common, pick it over most creatures.
Garrison Excavator: Strong playable. The near-zero IWD next to a 56.8% GIH means it's a low-variance value engine — it doesn't swing games but consistently makes your deck better. Mono-R castable, so the WR is real; solid in any red deck, great in Lorehold.
Rubble Rouser: Premium red common when your deck can use it — the experts nail the caveat. The 1/4 brick-wall plus card-filtering and graveyard-tap is exactly what grindy Prismari/soup wants; the 57.9% is real (mono-R) but conditional on having spells/yard payoffs to exploit.
Ambitious Augmenter: Premium — the +8.6 IWD and 2.1 ALSA on a mono-G rare mean this is a snowball bomb that transfers cleanly, no soup inflation. A counter engine that replaces itself with a fractal on death; a real reason to be green and a high pick anywhere.
Infirmary Healer: Filler. 53.7% with near-zero IWD and a soup-leaning OH/GD split — it's a fine green body that fixes/stabilizes but doesn't push damage. Playable role-player in midrange green, not a draw.
Thornfist Striker: Solid filler/role-player. 56.0% with positive IWD on a mono-G uncommon — castable WR that's a touch better than its 4.9 ALSA suggests. A proactive green creature that fits aggressive Witherbloom or a green-base soup; honest value.
Blech, Loafing Pest: Strong gold rare, but read the home — the 59.2% and +7.2 IWD are partly inflated by BG-into-soup pilots since Witherbloom is the format's worst clean archetype. Excellent engine in 5c soup, merely good in a real BG deck. High pick when you're already open.
Fix What's Broken: The experts are right — it's a trap that reads better than it plays (46.6% WR, 18.7% play rate is damning). The 'forgot or less' clause means it whiffs constantly outside a hyper-low-curve build. Sideboard/unplayable; skip it.
Lorehold Charm: The experts have it right — one of the weaker charms, and the negative IWD confirms it underperforms its modes. The 53.6% is soup-padded by flexible pilots; in a clean WR deck it's a marginal trick. Playable filler at best, not a high pick.
Prismari, the Inspiration: Trap-flavored mythic — the 6-point OH/GD gap (48.7 vs 57.3) screams 'dead in your opening hand, great once you're ahead.' The 54.4% overall is soup-pilots making it work; in 2c Prismari it's a clunky win-more. Mid pick, not the slam its rarity suggests.
Scolding Administrator: Premium gold uncommon and the 63% OH WR proves it's a proactive snowball, not a soup-only payoff — it wins games on curve. Silverquill's assertive plan loves it; menace + counter-transfer grows out of burn range. High pick, take it early.
Suspend Aggression: Strong playable. 57.4% with positive IWD and a 73% play rate — a flexible WR rare trick that does real work. The WR is mostly honest (Lorehold/Silverquill homes), not soup-juiced. A reliable high pick for proactive white decks.
Diary of Dreams: The experts are right to bury it — 51.7% with the OH/GD split (48.7 vs 53.5) shows it's a do-nothing early and a frequent late cut. Needs a permanent-dense engine deck it rarely finds in Limited. Sideboard; don't maindeck it.
Shattered Sanctum: Premium fixing land — the 57.3% WR and 3.8 ALSA make this near-mandatory in a Converge/soup format that lives and dies on mana. Effectively colorless utility; take it highly in any WB-adjacent or 5c deck. The WR is real, fixing wins this format.
Hop to It: Filler. The negative IWD next to 55.4% says it's a fine go-wide enabler that doesn't independently win games — its value rides on the rest of your board. Playable in token/aggressive white, replaceable otherwise.
Flusterstorm: Sideboard niche — 47.9% GIH and a 25.8% play rate (with zero OH/GD samples) confirm it's too narrow for maindeck Limited. A constructed reprint that whiffs when opponents aren't storming. Leave it in the board unless you're a dedicated control mirror.
Living End: Unplayable in this Limited — 3.2% play rate and no win-rate data means almost no one ran it, and it's a constructed combo card with no payoff here. Hard pass.
Crackle with Power: Premium soup bomb — but flag the inflation: the 65% GD WR reflects X=4-5 casts by 5c pilots, and the OH/GD gap (55.1 vs 65.0) shows it does nothing small. In a clean 2c deck it's a much worse Lava Coil. Slam it in soup, discount it elsewhere.
Knockout Maneuver: Premium green removal, agreed — +4.9 IWD and a clean mono-G pip mean the 56.7% transfers honestly. Unconditional-ish removal in a color that's usually short on it; take it over the fiddlier Withering Curse and over most creatures.
Archaeomancer: Soup-conditional payoff — the huge OH/GD gap (47.9 vs 57.9) and +4.6 IWD say it's dead early but a value engine once you have spells to rebuy. Mono-U castable, but the WR rides on a spell-dense Prismari/soup shell. Good there, mediocre in an aggressive deck.
Daydream: 49% GIH and negative IWD confirm the trap: a blink-with-flashback that needs strong ETBs SOS rarely offers, and it fights your own Repartee counters. Sideboard/unplayable outside a dedicated blink build that doesn't exist here.
Inkshape Demonstrator: 56.7% with honest mono-white pips means this transfers cleanly, no soup inflation. A 3/4 ward-2 that pumps and gains life is a premium Silverquill body and a strong early pick in any white aggro/tempo deck.
Spiritcall Enthusiast: 52% and negative IWD are underwhelming because it's archetype-locked: only worth it in Lorehold/Glassright token-and-treasure shells where it snowballs. Solid role-player there, near-unplayable filler everywhere else.
Emeritus of Ideation: 64.3% GIH, 14.4 IWD, ALSA 1.4 — this is a slam-dunk bomb, not soup-inflated since a 3UU 4/4 with Ancestral Recall stapled on wins on its own. Premier first-pick, take it over almost anything.
Mana Sculpt: The GD-OH split (60.1 vs 53.4) marks this as a build-around payoff that's mediocre off the top but elite once your deck is assembled — classic ramp/converge enabler. Strong pick for soup; just don't expect it to carry early.
Tester of the Tangential: 53.9% and positive IWD make this honest, castable Prismari/control filler — a fine spell-density body that holds the fort. Solid playable, not a priority; take it mid-pack to round out a blue deck.
End of the Hunt: 58.3% GIH with a low 3.3 ALSA and strong play rate flags premium removal masquerading as a common-ish uncommon. The GD>OH split says it's better as your deck cohere; near-format-staple black interaction, prioritize it.
Postmortem Professor: 60.1% and OH 63.5% (better in opener than drawn) make this a proactive black rare that wants to come down early and grind value. Premium pick and a real reason to be in black graveyard decks; pushes back on Witherbloom being unplayable.
Withering Curse: 53.4% is light for a mythic because conditional -2/-2 is a soft wrath that whiffs on big bodies; the destroy mode needs lifegain setup. Strong but not bomb-tier sweeper — high pick in lifegain-leaning Witherbloom/Silverquill, role-player elsewhere.
Goblin Glasswright: 53.8% with honest mono-red pips is exactly what it looks like: a serviceable treasure-making common that fuels Converge and Lorehold. Solid filler and a real soup enabler; nothing to prioritize but always playable.
Seize the Spoils: 55% but ALSA 6.3 and low play rate mean it's underdrafted card advantage — discard-for-two-cards-and-treasure is a quiet soup/control workhorse. Strong value at a late wheel; pick higher than its table position suggests.
Burrog Barrage: 55.2% with a healthy GD>OH split marks reliable green instant-speed interaction that scales with your board. Strong playable for any green deck; better drawn than in opener, so a fine mid-pick combat trick/removal piece.
Lumaret's Favor: 53% GIH but an 18.6% play rate and ALSA 7.2 say this is a niche trick most pilots cut — survivorship makes the WR look fine. Sideboard/situational at best; only roster it when you need the specific effect.
Topiary Lecturer: 54.5% and a solid 4.2 IWD make this an honest green ramp/fixing body that quietly enables Converge soup. Strong filler — the kind of unglamorous fixing 5c decks want; pick it up mid-pack without hesitation.
Bogwater Lumaret: 55.1% is genuinely good for a Witherbloom common and a small rebuttal to BG being the worst archetype — it's a sticky value frog. Solid playable that holds up the format's weakest pair; reasonable early-to-mid pick in BG.
Fractal Mascot: 54.1% overall but a stark OH 49.5 vs GD 56.7 split: weak topdeck, great with copy/Echo synergy as Numot notes. Soup/Quandrix payoff that needs support — strong in the right UG shell, mediocre as a standalone pick.
Lorehold, the Historian: 57.8% with ALSA 1.5 is strong but the multicolor WR is soup-pilot-flattered — in a clean Lorehold deck this Elder Dragon is a genuine bomb. Premium first-pick when you're already RW; otherwise a strong signal to move in.
Proctor's Gaze: 57.8% and 6.2 IWD make this excellent flexible UG instant interaction, with the GD>OH split showing it shines as your deck develops. Strong playable bordering premium for Quandrix/soup; high pick.
Silverquill Charm: 59.6% backs LR's "best charm" call — three relevant modes (counters/exile/drain) at instant speed is premium WB flexibility. Honest two-color WR that transfers; a top-tier Silverquill pick, take it early.
Tam, Observant Sequencer: 52.4% with a poor OH 48.8 shows it's clunky in your opener and needs board state to matter — a build-around legend that underdelivers. Filler-to-role-player in UG; don't overrate the legendary status, it's a mid-pick.
Mage Tower Referee: 53.8% but ALSA 7.4 and 27.8% play rate mean it's a colorless filler 2/2 most decks skip. Honest WR since it's cleanly castable, but it's pure curve-filler — sideboard/last-resort body only when desperate.
Skycoach Waypoint: Sub-50 GIH, negative IWD, and a 14% play rate gut LR's enthusiasm — a tapland that "prepares" rarely earns its slot in this format. Sideboard/unplayable; the data flatly contradicts the hype.
Prismatic Ending: 57% with honest white pips is premium flexible removal — Converge makes it scale beautifully in soup, but it's clean enough to transfer to two-color decks too. Strong-to-premium pick; one of white's better answers.
Force of Will: 56.7% but the OH 52 vs GD 59.4 gap and 2.5 DS reveal the catch: alt-cost is a card-disadvantage trap that's poor in your opener and best drawn with mana up. Strong in dedicated control/soup, overrated as a high pick — context-dependent.
Locust Spray: 52.1%, negative IWD, and a 27% play rate mark this as marginal cheap interaction most decks cut. Sideboard/situational removal at best; roster it only when you specifically need the cheap answer.
Empty the Warrens: 55.2% with a big 7.2 IWD but only 40% play rate — a storm-style payoff that's feast-or-famine, huge in spell-dense Prismari, dead elsewhere. Soup/spells-matter build-around; strong in the right deck, otherwise a sideboard rare.
Pick Your Poison: 47.5% GIH and a 5.6% play rate make this one of the batch's clear cuts — modal artifact/enchantment removal too narrow for SOS's threat base. Sideboard-only tech against specific permanents; not maindeckable.
Archmage Emeritus: 49.5% and negative IWD are a damning verdict on a marquee rare — magecraft needs a spell density SOS creatures don't support, and a 2/2 body dies easily. Overrated build-around; only a real card in a dedicated Prismari spells deck, fringe otherwise.
Dig Site Inventory: Honest mono-W common (cleanly castable, WR transfers): 55.4% on a 67% play rate is solid card advantage that any white deck wants. Negative IWD means it's velocity, not a swing card — strong filler/role-player, not a bomb.
Interjection: 55.7% with a flat IWD on only 29% play rate — the WR is real but it's a niche combat/protection trick that the better builds skip. Solid playable in Silverquill/Lorehold tempo, otherwise sideboard fodder.
Stand Up for Yourself: Premium white removal: 3-mana instant kill on power 3+ with +1.1 IWD and a 3.9 ALSA on an 86% play rate. Cleanly castable, so the 57% transfers fully — first-pick-caliber answer for any proactive white deck.
Encouraging Aviator: The soup trap exposed: 50.4% GIH and a 47.9% OH despite the glowing Opus note — a 2-toughness Prismari enabler that dies to everything and doesn't impact the board. Marginal; the repeatable trigger doesn't save a body this fragile.
Mathemagics: Format-defining soup bomb — 61.9% GIH, +12.9 IWD, 2.4 ALSA. But note the OH/GD gap (53.5 vs 66.3): it's a build-around payoff that's dead in your opener and lethal once online. First-pick in 5c soup, weaker if you can't power the X.
Textbook Tabulator: +3.3 IWD on a low 30% play rate is the tell: a fine soup Opus-enabler that the better decks pass, but it overperforms when it makes the cut. Solid soup role-player, skippable filler in a clean 2-color shell.
Eternal Student: Believe the trap call — 54.3% GIH but a NEGATIVE 1.0 IWD means it doesn't actually win games it's in. A 'good Magic card that's homeless' in a format where small-ball midrange is dead. Take format-relevant cards over it.
Pox Plague: BBBBB symmetrical Pox in a Converge format is unbuildable — 0.5% play rate confirms nobody runs it. Hard pass, never maindeck. The five black pips alone disqualify it in a soup format.
Ancestral Anger: 52.9% on a tiny 16% play rate with flat IWD — a cantrip combat trick that's pure deck-filler the format mostly ignores. Last-pickable in spell-dense Prismari/Lorehold, otherwise sideboard.
Heated Argument: Don't overrate the 55.5%/+2.7 IWD — 5-mana 6-damage burn is a slow finisher whose WR rides spell-heavy soup/Prismari shells. The 51.9% OH (you don't want it early) and LSV's cost gripe are right. Solid mid-pick removal, not a priority.
Steal the Show: Note the OH/GD split (49.9 vs 57.0): dead early, excellent late as a graveyard-fueled finisher — a build-around that scales with instants/sorceries. Strong in Prismari/Lorehold spell decks, mediocre elsewhere despite the rare tag.
Chelonian Tackle: Negative 1.5 IWD and a 47.5% OH — Witherbloom-adjacent green fight spell that's a liability more than an answer. Worst-archetype filler; sideboard it.
Mindful Biomancer: Vanilla-ish green common: 53.3% with flat IWD is honest, cleanly-castable filler that holds the ground and enables Opus/soup. Fine 23rd-card body, never a pick you prioritize.
Vastlands Scavenger: Premium green rare — 1GG 4/4 deathtouch is already a clean beater, and the Bind to Life prepare gives it reach/recursion. +4.9 IWD, 2.2 ALSA, cleanly castable so the 57% is real. Early pick that anchors any green deck.
Borrowed Knowledge: Lorehold-color but a 46.4% GIH and 3.2% play rate — functionally unplayable. Negative 2.4 IWD seals it; this is a sideboard/cut even in WR.
Fractal Tender: Numot's right — 'fine but unexciting.' 53.2% with +3.3 IWD is solid soup glue (Simic ramp/Opus enabler), but the OH/GD gap shows it needs the deck around it. Good role-player in 5c, not a payoff.
Mind into Matter: Excellent soup finisher — XGU draws X AND cheats a permanent in. 57.9% with +8 IWD, but the OH/GD split (53.9 vs 60.4) flags it as a build-around dead in openers. First-pick-caliber in 5c ramp, weak without big X.
Professor Dellian Fel: The best card in the batch — 62.5% GIH, +11.6 IWD, 1.4 ALSA, 60% OH. A planeswalker that's a bomb even in your opener (unlike most soup payoffs). Don't be fooled by the BG color identity; this is a slam first-pick that drags Witherbloom up by itself.
Silverquill, the Disputant: Underwhelming for a mythic dragon — 54.9% with only +1.9 IWD and a flat OH/GD. A fine WB Repartee aggro/tempo top-end but doesn't take over games the way the elder-dragon pedigree suggests. Strong playable, not a slam.
Teacher's Pest: Note the inverted OH>GD (55.3 vs 54.0) — it's better early as a proactive 2-drop than late, unusual for BG. A genuinely good aggressive Pest that helps Witherbloom actually function. Solid early pick, one of the better cards in the worst archetype.
Page, Loose Leaf: Colorless 2-mana artifact body, but a 15.9% play rate and 8.0 ALSA say the format rarely wants it. 52.7% is honest (mono-pip), so it's a fine fill-the-curve artifact deck piece — otherwise a late grab.
Spectacle Summit: Excellent fixing for a soup format — 56.4% GIH, +3.7 IWD, 84% play rate. Lands that enable Converge are gold here; this is a high pick precisely because the best deck is 5-color and ALWAYS wants more fixing.
Repel Calamity: Push back on LoL's 'underwhelmed' — 59% GIH, +3.2 IWD, 3.4 ALSA, 90% play rate on a cleanly-castable white instant. The stale-data caveat shows: this is premium, near-first-pick removal/protection, not merely 'fine.'
Pongify: Blue rare removal that's a trap here — 47.4% GIH because giving a 3/3 in a soup format full of bombs and Opus payoffs is a real downside. Conditional sideboard answer, not a maindeck pick despite hitting anything.
Sheoldred's Edict: Format-warped down — 53.0% with flat IWD and an inverted OH>GD. Edicts are mediocre when opponents flood the board with tokens/Fractals; it's a fine answer to bombs but unreliable. Solid role-player, not the premium it'd be elsewhere.
Jeska's Will: Insufficient data (0 WR, 12% play rate) — a powerful ritual/burst mythic that's narrow in 40-card Limited. Likely a niche Prismari/spell payoff; pick speculatively but don't expect a bomb. Treat as a flex playable.
Royal Treatment: Strong green protection trick — 56.1% with +4.2 IWD, cleanly castable so it transfers. Hexproof-and-counters-removal at instant speed defends your soup bombs perfectly. Solid-to-strong playable, a key answer to the format's removal.
Murmuring Mystic: Premium blue uncommon — 56.4% GIH, +4.8 IWD, 2.7 ALSA, 90% play rate. The token engine is gas in spell-dense Prismari/soup and the WR is honestly earned. Early pick; one of the best blue commons-slot cards here.
Eager Glyphmage: The experts are right: a 3W 3/3 + 1/1 inkling is honest filler, and its 56% WR transfers cleanly without soup inflation. Fine roleplayer that's best in Silverquill where the inkling feeds Repartee, but never the powerhouse the prerelease hype promised. Mid-pack body.
Joined Researchers: Pedestrian rare: a 54.9% WR at 73.9% play is a curve-filler that high pick-rate flatters. Cleanly castable so the number holds, but it's a role-player you take, not a card you raise the white flag for. Solid, unexciting.
Stirring Hopesinger: First-pick bomb, full stop. 62.8% GIH / 7.9 IWD / 1.6 ALSA all scream premium, and this is mono-W honest data, not soup inflation. A 2W lifelink flier that anthems your board on every Repartee is an engine in any white deck and absurd in Silverquill.
Essence Scatter: Strong playable counter, but the 56.9% is partly soup-pilot polish; in a tight 2-color creature-light field it's deader. Format skews low-creature, so play it as a flexible interaction piece in Prismari/spell decks and sideboard it up vs. creature-heavy hands. Don't pass into open mana.
Matterbending Mage: Quietly excellent: 58.1% GIH with a 60.1% GD shows it overperforms once it sticks. Spell-deck payoff that rewards Prismari/soup density, so some inflation, but it's an uncommon you're happy to wheel into. Strong playable, prioritize in a control shell.
Wisdom of Ages: Textbook soup-mirror bomb: 61.6% GD vs. a 50% OH lays the inflation bare — it does nothing on an empty board, then takes over a grind. Premium ONLY in control/Prismari with card-velocity payoffs; in clean 2-color aggro it's a near-trap. Draft the deck for it.
Foolish Fate: Premium black removal hiding in plain sight. 58.6% GIH, 92% play, 2.7 ALSA — an instant that handles nearly anything, and the WR is mono-B honest. First-pick-quality interaction; take it over most creatures and snap it whenever it shows.
Pull from the Grave: The experts nail it: first copy is a fine value sorcery, but the 52.6% / 30.7% play and below-zero IWD show it's clunky and slow. Recursion only matters in grindy soup, and it's dead in aggressive draws. A late-pick role-player, not a maindeck staple.
Archaic's Agony: The 56.3% GIH masks a split: 58.9% GD vs. 51.5% OH means it's a board-state-dependent sorcery that's weak in your opener. Soup/grind payoff that the 48% play rate confirms is niche. Solid when your deck wants it, otherwise a sideboard card.
Impractical Joke: Premium cheap red removal — 3 damage at instant-ish value, 58.6% GIH at a massive 93% play, and it's mono-R honest data. Cheap removal is king in this soup format where you need to interact across five colors. High pick, take early.
Strife Scholar: Replacement-level body. 50.8% GIH, sub-zero IWD, 24.9% play and a 2.1 Draftsim all agree this is bottom-of-pool. Plays only when you're desperate for bodies; cut it the moment a real card shows up. Sideboard/last-pick.
Comforting Counsel: Genuine trap rare. 45.4% GIH and -6.3 IWD are damning, and a 1.0 Draftsim confirms nobody should touch it. The build-around payoff never arrives before you've lost. Hard pass even in dedicated green decks; it actively loses you games.
Noxious Newt: Honest, useful green common — 55.4% GIH that transfers cleanly with 84% play. A deathtouch-flavored body that trades up and fits any green deck, including Witherbloom where the format is otherwise barren. Solid filler you're glad to run, prioritize as roleplayer glue.
Wild Hypothesis: Marginal: 52.2% with a telling 4.3-point OH/GD gap (48.3 vs. 54.6) means it's a late-game value sorcery that's dead early and only shines once you're set up. 31% play confirms niche. Soup/green-ramp filler, not a card you reach for.
Cauldron of Essence: The 54.9% is GD-inflated (57.1 vs. 51.8 OH) — a slow Recurring-Nightmare engine that needs creatures and a board, exactly what Numot's non-bo warning flags. It's a Witherbloom (the worst archetype) build-around. Cheap ALSA but a trap outside a dedicated sacrifice deck.
Geometer's Arthropod: Underwhelming UG rare: 54.5% with a flat OH/GD split and only 40% play means it's a fine body that doesn't move the needle. Fractal/counters payoff that needs the Quandrix soup shell. Mid-pick role-player, not a card you prioritize into your colors.
Mind Roots: The 52.8% modest WR fits Numot's read — opponents can dodge the discard, so it's swingy. The +1/+1 counters give it floor as a creature deck card, but the 56.6% play and tiny IWD say solid-not-special. Witherbloom filler; fine, never exciting.
Pterafractyl: Honest soup payoff — 53.5% overall but a revealing 48.9% OH vs. 56.2% GD shows it's a dead draw early and a scaling threat late. XGU flier wants the Converge/ramp shell. Solid in 5c soup, weak in a clean 2-color deck where you can't reliably hit big X.
Snooping Page: Strong Silverquill tempo two-drop — evasive Repartee body that draws, and the 55.9% with a 57% OH (rare for it to be higher) signals it's good early and on the play. The experts respect it. Prioritize in WB aggro/tempo; honest, not soup-inflated.
Traumatic Critique: Premium first-pick removal — 60.4% GIH, a format-leading 10.0 IWD, 2.0 ALSA. Scalable kill-plus-card-advantage that's a Prismari finisher; the WR is somewhat soup-flattered (62.6 GD), but even floored as a 2-mana kill it's elite. Snap it every time.
Potioner's Trove: The format keystone — take it over almost everything once you have any spell payoff. A 3-mana any-color rock that fixes the 5c soup AND gains life to dodge burn is the glue that reorders the entire draft. 57.4% undersells how much it enables. Premium, period.
Stormcarved Coast: Premium fixing land — 57% GIH and 84% play with effectively no opportunity cost, since dual lands are free wins in a Converge soup format. Take it highly; it enables your splashes and the WR is honest (lands don't soup-inflate). Near-auto-include in UR/Prismari.
Reprieve: Strong soup-mirror overperformer, as Numot flags — a cheap tempo/protection trick that 'blinks' a threat or saves your bomb and buys a draw step. 58.9% holds across a clean spell deck. Prioritize in Prismari control where it protects engines; flexible and slept-on.
Preordain: Reliable premium cantrip — 56.2% GIH that's mostly honest (mild GD lean) and a backbone of any spell-density deck. Smooths your soup draws and fuels instant/sorcery payoffs like the Trove. Strong playable; take early in control, fine in anything blue.
Smallpox: No data (5.1% play, all-zero WR) means the field correctly never plays it — symmetric sacrifice/discard is a build-around with no home in this creature-and-card-advantage soup format. Sideboard/unplayable. Don't experiment; the silence is the verdict.
Monstrous Rage: Solid combat trick — 56.2% GIH but a near-zero 1.6 IWD shows it's high-variance, blowout when it lands, dead in hand otherwise. The 56% play undersells it slightly. Best in proactive Lorehold/aggro decks that force favorable combat; a fine role-player, not a staple.
Shamanic Revelation: Coin-flip rare — 50.0% GIH, negative IWD, 34.6% play. A board-wide draw payoff that needs a wide green deck the format doesn't reliably support, and it's a blank when behind. Niche soup/go-wide card; mostly a sideboard rare you skip for proactive picks.
Grim Haruspex: Good engine card flattered by its low ALSA — 54.0% GIH with a sub-zero IWD reveals it's not as backbreaking as a 2.7 first-pick suggests. Card-draw-on-death payoff that wants Witherbloom sacrifice, the worst archetype. Strong playable in the right deck, overrated as a raw pick.
Elite Interceptor: Believe the 59% WR — a clean mono-W 1-drop that draws and disrupts transfers honestly to any deck, no soup inflation. Premier white common and a real first-pick; the Rejoinder tap-untap synergy is gravy on an already top-tier floor.
Owlin Historian: Negative IWD and a 6.2 ALSA say this is a body-and-value role-player, not a deck-driver; the 55% WR is honest mono-W. Solid mid-pick filler that plays fine anywhere but you're never thrilled to first-pick it.
Stone Docent: A 1W 3/1 that asks nothing — cleanly castable, so the 56.5% WR is real and transfers to every white deck. Negative IWD flags it as a curve-filler, not a payoff: aggressive role-player, great early Silverquill/Lorehold beater.
Exhibition Tidecaller: Massive 9.8 IWD and 60%+ WR are real, but this is a build-around the data underrates if you misplay it — LR is right, hold it past turn 6. Premium blue rare, strongest in spell-dense Prismari; first-pick that punishes impatience.
Muse Seeker: The 52.6% WR is honest and underwhelming — a 2-toughness body that needs a 5-mana trigger dies before it loots in a removal-heavy format. Marginal playable; only run it in dedicated big-spell Prismari and even then it's near-last.
Adventurous Eater: Negative IWD and 54% WR mark this as a pure curve-filler body — cleanly mono-B so the number transfers honestly, just nothing exciting. Replaceable role-player; fine in any black deck but the first cut when your pool gets deep.
Forum Necroscribe: A 5B 5/4 that underdelivers — 51% WR and negative IWD confirm a vanilla beater whose Warlock text rarely matters. Sub-replacement; sideboard/last-playable even in Witherbloom, the format's worst archetype where it has no home.
Rabid Attack: Classic trap: reads fine, plays at 50% with negative IWD and a 22% play rate the data already exiled it. Conditional combat trick that costs tempo — sideboard at best, leave it in the pack.
Artistic Process: 57% WR with a clean single red pip makes this an honest strong playable, not soup-inflated — red's flexible answer to fatties. High pick for Prismari/any red deck; the Slumbering Trudge mode is exactly the removal red usually lacks.
Improvisation Capstone: Elite — 62.6% WR, 11.2 IWD, 1.8 ALSA all scream bomb, and as a colorless-payload-but-RR sorcery the number is honest for any red deck. Premium first-pick that buries opponents in card advantage; take it over almost anything.
Tablet of Discovery: 56.8% WR honest — a 2R rock that fixes for spells, best when you know you're UR. LR nails it: take Potioner's Trove early for flexibility, slot Tablet once committed to Prismari. Strong playable enabler, not a payoff.
Efflorescence: Dead card — 49% WR, 8.2 ALSA, 6.7% play rate. The data is unanimous: this never makes the deck. Pure sideboard/unplayable, even in green.
Oracle's Restoration: 49% WR and a 14% play rate — a too-slow green sorcery the field correctly ignores. Unplayable outside the narrowest graveyard build; leave it in the pack.
Wildgrowth Archaic: The 56% WR hides a bimodal card — LoL is right, it's strong as a T2 GG curve-out and a mediocre 4-mana 2/2 anywhere else. Strong only in dedicated aggressive green; speculative pick that needs the early-GG home to earn its rate.
Colorstorm Stallion: Bomb-tier UR rare — 62.5% WR and 12.0 IWD, and while gold cards get soup inflation, this wins games in clean Prismari too. Premium first-pick; the best 2-color shell's payoff and a slam at P1P1.
Grapple with Death: 56.4% flexible removal, but the gold WR is soup-friendly — in a true BG (the worst pair) it's still good because destroy-anything + lifegain is just efficient. Strong playable, the rare reason to be in Witherbloom; high pick when committed.
Molten Note: Premium Lorehold removal-with-reach — 59.5% WR, and as a real RW proactive deck (not soup), this number is trustworthy. High pick that pushes damage while killing; one of the best uncommons for the format's best aggro pair.
Pursue the Past: Don't sleep — LR's #1-common-by-winrate is real: 59% WR, and the flashback card-advantage engine drives the genuinely proactive Lorehold deck (not soup-inflated). Premium common, take it highly; it's the RW value motor, not a throwaway.
Social Snub: 51% WR and an 11% play rate — a narrow WB sorcery the field rightly skips. Sideboard tech at most, even in Silverquill; leave it in the pack.
Vibrant Outburst: 58.7% WR and it can hit face — a premium Prismari uncommon that kills and tempos simultaneously. The gold rate gets mild soup help but UR control wants exactly this; high pick, often a reach-finisher in clean Prismari.
Strixhaven Skycoach: Colorless 59% WR transfers everywhere — a two-for-one that smooths land drops and dodges counters. LoL's caveat matters: it sags below ~10 creatures to crew, so prioritize in bodied decks. Strong playable, broadly splashable enabler.
Sundown Pass: 57% WR is a clean dual-land floor — free fixing that any WR/soup deck wants and the colorless number is fully honest. High pick for the mana, especially in 5c soup where fixing is king; never a wasted slot.
Requisition Raid: 50.7% WR, negative IWD, 23% play rate — a situational white sorcery the data dismisses. Sideboard/marginal; only relevant against specific permanents, otherwise cut.
Sleight of Hand: 55.8% WR with a healthy 4.9 IWD — honest mono-U card selection that smooths the spell decks. Solid playable, best in Prismari where finding your key spells matters; a quiet role-player that overperforms its reputation.
Stargaze: 55% WR is soup-flattered — a BB-X X-spell that pilots well in slow 5c shells but, as LR says, a clean 2-color deck has no time for it. Marginal/soup-only; 29% play rate confirms it's rarely worth the deck slot.
Pyretic Ritual: No 17Lands data (0 WR) — a 6.4% play-rate red ritual with no Limited home; mana acceleration into nothing here. Sideboard/unplayable; only a build-around combo piece would ever maindeck it.
Shared Roots: A bonus-sheet Rampant Growth as a Lesson — 55% WR and 4.2 IWD reflect honest fixing/ramp that's better than vanilla because it's a fetchable Lesson. Solid playable in green ramp and 5c soup, where the fixing earns its keep.
Dualcaster Mage: The 50.5% WR and ugly 45.8% OH-WR expose a do-nothing-if-you-have-no-spell trap — the 3.2 ALSA is name-tax hype, not performance. Marginal; only a dense Prismari deck makes it functional, and even then it's a weak rare.
